engineIntelligence.h2: Skoda Yeti Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Skoda Yeti in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Skoda Yeti CBZB engine - petrol 1.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure - causes rattling on startup and potential catastrophic engine damage.
Oil consumption - worn piston rings lead to excessive oil burn at higher mileages.

cards.altText: Skoda Yeti CBDB engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: DPF blockage - frequent short journeys cause particulate filter clogging and limp mode.
Turbocharger failure - wastegate rattle and boost leaks due to oil starvation.

cards.altText: Skoda Yeti CDAA engine - petrol 1.8L replacement UK
knownFailures: Serious engine failure - timing chain jump and piston damage reported at relatively low mileages.
High-pressure fuel pump failure - causes hard starting and engine cut-outs.

engineGuide.h2: Skoda Yeti Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Skoda Yeti across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The efficient common-rail diesel introduced to replace older PD units, offering smooth refinement and excellent fuel economy for the Yeti range.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 TDI, 1.6 TDI GreenLine, S, SE, Elegance
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging causing rough idle and limp mode (60k+ miles).
Turbocharger actuator sticking due to carbon buildup.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAYC Skoda Yeti engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel (140hp)
entries.history: The backbone of the Yeti diesel lineup, providing robust torque for the heavy crossover chassis and essential for 4x4 models.
commonFailures: DPF blockage from frequent short urban journeys (80k+ miles).
Dual-mass flywheel wear causing clutch shudder and rattling.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CBDB Skoda Yeti engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel (110hp)
entries.history: The lower-output variant of the 2.0 TDI, tuned for maximum fuel efficiency and lower emissions in standard FWD models.
commonFailures: Injector seal leaks leading to oil contamination and diesel smell.
Timing chain stretch on early common-rail conversions.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CFFB Skoda Yeti engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel (170hp)
entries.history: The range-topping performance diesel, exclusively paired with the 4x4 drivetrain and higher-spec trims for maximum towing and off-road capability.
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump failure causing starting issues.
Turbocharger failure due to high boost pressures and oil coking.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CFGB Skoda Yeti engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: The volume-selling petrol engine for the Yeti, offering a balance of low-end torque and efficiency, but plagued by timing chain vulnerabilities.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure causing rattling and potential engine seizure (60k-90k miles).
Excessive oil consumption due to faulty piston ring design.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CBZB Skoda Yeti engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The twin-charged (supercharger and turbo) petrol option, delivering strong performance but requiring meticulous maintenance to avoid internal wear.
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves causing misfires and power loss.
Water pump failure leading to overheating and head gasket damage.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAXA Skoda Yeti engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: The flagship petrol engine reserved for 4x4 and top-tier trims, providing ample power but suffering from well-documented early-stage failures.
commonFailures: Catastrophic engine failure due to timing chain jump and piston damage (45k+ miles).
High oil consumption requiring frequent top-ups between services.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CDAA Skoda Yeti engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Skoda Yeti Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Skoda Yeti’s engine lineup is generally robust, but specific vulnerabilities emerge as mileage increases. The 1.2 TSI timing chain failure and 1.8 TSI catastrophic engine damage dominate UK replacement demand, alongside 2.0 TDI DPF issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address these original failure points - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this guide to weigh your repair versus replacement options.

problems.h4: 1.2 TSI Timing Chain Failure - Why Skoda Yeti Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: The original timing chain tensioner and plastic guides wear prematurely, allowing the chain to stretch, rattle, and potentially skip a tooth, causing valve-to-piston contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing the chain, tensioner, and guides before failure occurs.
repairOptions.longevity: Highly recommended preventative maintenance around 60k miles.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fitting a reconditioned 1.2 TSI engine with an upgraded timing kit.
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if the chain has already jumped and caused internal damage.

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattling noise on cold startup, act immediately. A preventative timing kit replacement is far cheaper than a full engine replacement, but if the engine has suffered interference damage, a rebuilt unit is your safest investment.
problems.cta: Find preventative and rebuilt 1.2 TSI engine options for your Yeti

problems.h4: 1.8 TSI Catastrophic Engine Failure - Why Skoda Yeti Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: A known design flaw in the early EA888 gen 1 engines causes the timing chain to jump or snap, leading to severe internal damage, piston cracking, and complete engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing pistons, valves, and timing components without a full short block.
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if the cylinder block and crankshaft remain undamaged.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Installing a fully rebuilt 1.8 TSI engine with updated gen 2 timing components.
repairOptions.longevity: The most reliable path for high-mileage units to avoid repeat failures.

problems.recommendation: Once a 1.8 TSI suffers a timing chain jump, the internal damage is often catastrophic. We strongly recommend a full rebuilt engine with upgraded gen 2 timing components over a partial top-end repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1.8 TSI engine prices for your Skoda Yeti

problems.h4: 2.0 TDI DPF & Turbo Issues - Why Skoda Yeti Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Skoda Yeti 2.0 TDI (2009-2017, CBDB, CFHC, CEGA, CFGB)
problems.rootCause: Frequent short journeys prevent the Diesel Particulate Filter (DPF) from regenerating, causing excessive backpressure that damages the turbocharger and leads to limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical cleaning of DPF and replacing turbo actuator seals.
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; blockage often returns if driving habits don't change.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fitting a reconditioned 2.0 TDI engine with a refurbished DPF and turbo.
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution restoring full power and MOT compliance.

problems.recommendation: If the engine is otherwise healthy, professional DPF cleaning is a smart preventative measure. However, if the turbo has failed due to DPF backpressure, metal debris may have entered the engine, necessitating a full replacement.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 2.0 TDI engine replacement for your Skoda Yeti

problems.h4: 1.4 TSI Oil Consumption & Carbon Buildup - Why Skoda Yeti Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Direct injection leads to severe carbon buildup on intake valves, while worn piston rings cause excessive oil consumption, eventually leading to catalytic converter damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Walnut blasting intake valves and replacing piston rings.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if the cylinder walls are not severely scored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Installing a reconditioned 1.4 TSI engine with updated oil control rings.
repairOptions.longevity: The definitive solution for an engine burning over 1 litre per 1,000 miles.

problems.recommendation: If your 1.4 TSI is consuming excessive oil, do not ignore it. A rebuilt engine with updated piston rings is the only permanent fix to prevent catalytic converter failure and engine seizure.
problems.cta: Find a reliable replacement engine for your Skoda Yeti

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Skoda Yeti
finalCta.paragraph: Ignoring early warning signs like timing chain rattle, DPF limp mode, or excessive oil consumption can turn a manageable repair into a total engine loss. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Skoda Yeti specialists, ensuring you get a fair deal. All our network suppliers provide upgraded components to fix known factory flaws, nationwide delivery, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Skoda Yeti Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Skoda Yeti and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Skoda Yeti 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2-litre TSI was the volume-selling petrol engine for the Yeti, offering a balance of low-end torque and efficiency. While popular, it is heavily associated with timing chain tensioner failures and excessive oil consumption at higher mileages.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner failure causing rattling
items.cta: Compare Skoda Yeti 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Skoda Yeti 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4-litre TSI twin-charged engine provided strong performance for the Yeti crossover. It requires meticulous maintenance to avoid carbon buildup on the intake valves and water pump failures that can lead to overheating.
commonFailurePoints: water pump failure leading to overheating.
items.cta: Compare Skoda Yeti 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Skoda Yeti 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8-litre TSI was the flagship petrol engine, reserved for 4x4 and top-tier trims. It suffers from a well-documented design flaw in the early EA888 engines, leading to catastrophic timing chain failures at relatively low mileages.
commonFailurePoints: Catastrophic engine failure due to timing chain jump
items.cta: Compare Skoda Yeti 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Skoda Yeti 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6-litre TDI common-rail diesel was introduced to replace older PD units, offering smooth refinement and excellent fuel economy, particularly in the GreenLine variants. It is generally reliable but prone to EGR clogging on urban-driven examples.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve clogging causing rough idle

items.title: Skoda Yeti 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre TDI is the backbone of the Yeti diesel lineup, providing robust torque essential for the heavy crossover chassis and 4x4 models. High-mileage examples often suffer from DPF blockages and dual-mass flywheel wear.
commonFailurePoints: dual-mass flywheel wear causing clutch shudder.

items.title: Skoda Yeti Diesel Engines
items.description: The diesel story for the Yeti is dominated by the highly efficient TDI family, which accounts for the vast majority of UK registrations and replacement enquiries, especially in 4x4 and Outdoor variants.

items.familiesLabel: Common Skoda Yeti Diesel Engine Families
families: CAYC - 1.6 TDI 105hp, efficient FWD option, prone to EGR clogging.
CBDB/CFHC - 2.0 TDI 140hp, the volume seller, robust but suffers from DPF issues.
CFGB - 2.0 TDI 170hp, the performance 4x4 unit, high torque but turbo wear at high mileage.
knownFor: Excellent high-mileage motorway fuel economy and strong towing capability.
DPF blockage issues on cars used primarily for short urban journeys.
High UK replacement demand on 2.0 TDI 140 4x4 and Outdoor variants.

engineYears.h2: Skoda Yeti Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: Use this Skoda Yeti year guide to match your vehicle to the right engine family, production period, and replacement options.

years.description: The launch of the Skoda Yeti introduced a unique crossover SUV to the UK market, initially powered by the robust 2.0 TDI PD and early common-rail petrol engines.
keyChanges: Introduction of the Skoda Yeti to the UK market as a practical, rugged crossover.
Standard fitment of 1.2 TSI, 1.4 TSI, and 2.0 TDI 140 engines.
Establishment of S, SE, and Elegance trim levels.
knownFor: Practical, boxy design with excellent interior space and visibility.
Early 1.2 TSI units exhibiting the first wave of timing chain tensioner wear.
High demand for the 2.0 TDI 4x4 for light off-road and towing duties.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2012 Skoda Yeti replacement engines
years.ticker: 2010 Skoda Yeti 1.2 TSI engine replacement • 2011 Yeti 2.0 TDI timing chain fix

years.description: The 2013 facelift introduced the Outdoor range, refined engine management software, and the powerful 2.0 TDI 170 4x4, cementing the Yeti's reputation as a capable family SUV.
keyChanges: Launch of the Skoda Yeti Outdoor with rugged cladding and off-road styling.
Introduction of the 2.0 TDI 170 4x4 engine for maximum performance.
Refined ECU calibrations to improve emissions and fuel economy across the range.
knownFor: Best overall reliability of the Yeti lineup due to mid-production refinements.
High desirability for low-mileage, rebuilt 2.0 TDI 170 examples.
Strong cross-compatibility with VW Tiguan and Audi Q3 engines of the same era.
years.cta: Secure a warrantied 2013-2017 Skoda Yeti replacement engine today
years.ticker: 2014 Skoda Yeti 2.0 TDI Outdoor replacement • 2015 Yeti 1.6 TDI GreenLine engine fix

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Skoda Yeti was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Skoda Yeti specialists.

faq.h2: Skoda Yeti Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Skoda Yeti engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A Skoda Yeti engine replacement typically costs between £800 and £2,600 for a rebuilt unit, depending on whether you have the 1.2 TSI, 1.6 TDI, or 2.0 TDI. Used options start around £500, while full supply and fit packages will add £500-£900 in labour.
keyPoints: 1.2 TSI rebuilt engines average £800-£1,800.
1.6 TDI and 2.0 TDI rebuilt engines average £1,100-£2,600.
Supply and fit labour adds £500-£900.
All rebuilt units include a 12-month warranty.
items.cta: Compare Skoda Yeti engine replacement prices today

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Skoda Yeti?
items.answer: Yes, replacing the engine is highly viable and often more economical than purchasing another vehicle, given the Yeti's robust chassis and the affordability of reconditioned units.
keyPoints: A rebuilt engine costs significantly less than a new car.
The Yeti chassis and drivetrain are durable and long-lasting.
Reconditioned units fix factory flaws like timing chain risks.
Extends the vehicle's life by another 80,000+ miles.
items.cta: Request a Skoda Yeti rebuilt engine quote today

items.question: What is the most reliable engine for a Skoda Yeti?
items.answer: The 1.6 TDI (CAYC) and later 2.0 TDI 140 (CFHC) are widely regarded as the most robust and longest-lasting engine options for the Skoda Yeti, provided DPF maintenance is observed.
keyPoints: 1.6 TDI avoids the complex twin-charger issues of the 1.4 TSI.
2.0 TDI offers immense torque for the heavy Yeti chassis.
Both require regular oil changes to prevent timing chain wear.
Avoid the 1.8 TSI (CDAA) due to known catastrophic failures.
items.cta: Find a reconditioned 1.6 TDI Skoda Yeti engine with 12-month warranty

items.question: What causes the 1.2 TSI timing chain failure in the Skoda Yeti?
items.answer: Timing chain failure is primarily caused by a weak original tensioner and plastic guides that wear prematurely, allowing the chain to stretch and potentially skip a tooth, destroying the engine.
keyPoints: Listen for a rattling noise on cold startup.
Can lead to catastrophic valve-to-piston contact if ignored.
Upgraded timing chain kits are a common rebuild solution.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 1.2 TSI engine prices for your Skoda Yeti

items.question: Can I get a supply and fit service for a Yeti engine?
items.answer: Yes, we operate a nationwide network of vetted, specialist garages that handle the entire process, from sourcing the engine to professional installation and road testing.
keyPoints: Single point of contact for the entire replacement process.
Includes ECU reprogramming and system calibration.
Full parts and labour warranty for complete protection.
items.cta: Book Skoda Yeti engine supply and fit today

items.question: How do I find my Skoda Yeti engine code?
items.answer: Check the vehicle's V5C logbook, look for the stamped code on the engine block itself near the gearbox flange, or simply enter your registration number into our free online checker.
keyPoints: V5C logbook lists the engine capacity and sometimes the code.
Stamped metal plate on the engine block is the most accurate source.
Our DVLA-linked registration checker identifies it instantly.
items.cta: Get a Skoda Yeti engine compatibility check now

items.question: Does a Skoda Yeti engine replacement include a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every engine sourced through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Supply and fit packages often include comprehensive parts and labour coverage.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty on all rebuilt units.
Unlimited mileage coverage for total peace of mind.
Supply and fit includes labour warranty.
Warranty terms are clearly stated by each UK specialist.
items.cta: Get quotes for Skoda Yeti engine replacement with warranty

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned Skoda Yeti engines?
items.answer: While often used interchangeably, "reconditioned" typically implies a more thorough restoration to exact factory tolerances, whereas "rebuilt" may only replace specifically failed components.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engines are fully stripped and machined.
Rebuilt engines target specific failure points (e.g., timing chain).
Both options are vastly superior to untested used engines.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned Skoda Yeti engine prices

items.question: How long does Skoda Yeti engine supply and fit take?
items.answer: A typical Skoda Yeti engine supply and fit process takes 3 to 5 working days, covering sourcing, nationwide delivery, professional installation, and final ECU calibration.
keyPoints: Sourcing a matched engine takes 1-3 days.
Nationwide delivery adds 1-2 days.
Professional installation requires 1-2 days.
items.cta: Find a reliable replacement engine for your Skoda Yeti

items.question: Why is my Skoda Yeti 2.0 TDI in limp mode?
items.answer: Limp mode in the 2.0 TDI is most commonly caused by DPF blockage or turbocharger actuator failure due to frequent short journeys preventing proper regeneration.
keyPoints: Check for excessive black smoke or rough idling.
Professional cleaning can sometimes resolve the issue.
Severe blockages may require a reconditioned engine swap.
items.cta: Get quotes for 2.0 TDI engine replacement for your Skoda Yeti
